source: MITRE ATT&CK, CISA, FBI IC3, Industry Reports

attack_chains.attack_chain: Phishing email delivers loader malware, which establishes C2, performs lateral movement via SMB/RDP, then deploys ransomware with double-extortion data leak threat.
indicators: Unusual SMB traffic between workstations
Mass file encryption events
C2 beaconing to known ransomware infrastructure
Shadow copy deletion commands
attack_chains.business_impact: Average ransomware payment reached $1.54M in 2025; total recovery costs average $4.7M including downtime, legal, and remediation.
defensive_measures: Immutable offline backups with tested restoration
Network segmentation limiting lateral movement
EDR with behavioral ransomware detection
Email gateway with sandboxing for attachments

attack_chains.attack_chain: Attacker compromises email via credential phishing, monitors mailbox for pending transactions, then injects fraudulent wire instructions at the critical moment.
indicators: Inbox rules forwarding to external addresses
Login from anomalous geolocations
Subtle changes in reply-to addresses
Urgency language in payment requests
attack_chains.business_impact: FBI IC3 reports BEC losses exceeded $2.9B in 2024; average loss per incident is $125,000 with recovery rate below 30%.
defensive_measures: Mandatory verbal verification for all wire changes
Conditional access policies blocking legacy auth
DMARC/DKIM/SPF enforcement
Real-time mailbox rule monitoring
attack_chains.source: FBI IC3 Annual Report 2024, MITRE ATT&CK T1114

attack_chains.attack_chain: Adversary-in-the-middle phishing proxy captures MFA tokens and session cookies, enabling immediate account takeover bypassing traditional MFA.
indicators: Phishing pages proxying legitimate login portals
Session token reuse from different IPs
Impossible travel logins
Bulk mailbox access after credential harvest
attack_chains.business_impact: Compromised credentials are the initial vector in 49% of breaches; average breach cost attributable to stolen credentials is $4.6M.
defensive_measures: Phishing-resistant MFA such as FIDO2/WebAuthn
Continuous access evaluation policies
Credential monitoring on dark web marketplaces
Conditional access with device compliance checks

attack_chains.attack_chain: Attacker compromises a trusted vendor's software update mechanism or managed service portal to distribute malicious code to downstream customers.
indicators: Unexpected software update outside normal schedule
New processes spawned by trusted vendor tools
Anomalous outbound connections from vendor software
Code signing certificate mismatches
attack_chains.business_impact: Supply chain attacks affect an average of 1,000+ downstream organizations per incident; SolarWinds-style attacks cost affected firms $12M+ each in remediation.
defensive_measures: Vendor security assessment program with continuous monitoring
Application allowlisting blocking unauthorized executables
Network microsegmentation for vendor access
Software bill of materials validation
Zero-trust architecture for third-party connections

attack_chains.attack_chain: Disgruntled or compromised employee leverages legitimate access to exfiltrate sensitive data via cloud storage, USB, or personal email over weeks or months.
indicators: Bulk file downloads outside normal patterns
Access to data outside job role
USB device connections on restricted systems
After-hours access spikes before resignation
attack_chains.business_impact: Insider incidents cost an average of $15.4M annually per organization; data theft cases take an average of 85 days to contain.
defensive_measures: User behavior analytics with baseline profiling
Data loss prevention on endpoints and cloud apps
Privileged access management with session recording
Automated offboarding with immediate access revocation
attack_chains.source: Ponemon Cost of Insider Threats 2025, MITRE ATT&CK T1567

attack_chains.attack_chain: After initial access, attacker stages sensitive files in a compressed archive and exfiltrates via encrypted channels, DNS tunneling, or cloud storage APIs.
indicators: Large outbound data transfers to new destinations
DNS queries with high entropy subdomains
RAR/7z archives created in staging directories
Cloud API calls to unauthorized storage buckets
attack_chains.business_impact: Average cost of a data breach involving exfiltration is $4.88M; regulatory fines for healthcare/legal data can add $1-5M per incident.
defensive_measures: Network DLP inspecting encrypted traffic via TLS inspection
DNS monitoring for tunneling and DGA patterns
Cloud access security broker for shadow IT detection
Endpoint DLP blocking unauthorized file transfers
attack_chains.source: IBM Cost of a Data Breach 2025, MITRE ATT&CK T1041

attack_chains.attack_chain: Attacker exploits unpatched network-connected medical device to pivot into clinical network, accessing patient data or disrupting care delivery systems.
indicators: Scanning activity targeting medical device ports
Firmware mismatch on connected devices
Unusual network traffic from IoMT devices
Authentication bypass attempts on device management interfaces
attack_chains.business_impact: Medical device compromises can halt patient care costing $47,000 per hour of downtime; FDA recalls and liability exposure can exceed $10M.
defensive_measures: Medical device network segmentation with micro-perimeters
Continuous IoMT asset inventory and vulnerability scanning
Virtual patching via network IPS for legacy devices
Manufacturer disclosure monitoring and patch management
attack_chains.source: FDA Cybersecurity Guidance 2025, MITRE ATT&CK T1190

attack_chains.attack_chain: Targeted spear-phish delivers initial access, attacker escalates to domain admin via Kerberoasting or AD certificate abuse, achieving full enterprise compromise.
indicators: Kerberos TGS requests for service accounts
LDAP queries enumerating privileged groups
DCSync replication requests from non-DC hosts
Certificate template abuse in AD CS
attack_chains.business_impact: Full domain compromise enables total data access; average cost of an enterprise-wide breach exceeds $9.4M with 280+ day mean time to contain.
defensive_measures: Tiered administration model for Active Directory
Privileged access workstations for domain admins
AD certificate services hardening per CISA guidance
Continuous AD attack path analysis and reduction
LAPS or managed service account passwords
attack_chains.source: CISA Active Directory Security Guidance, MITRE ATT&CK T1558

social_engineering.attack_chain: Attacker impersonates CEO, managing partner, or chief medical officer to pressure subordinates into bypassing controls for urgent wire transfers or data access.
indicators: Email from lookalike domain
Requests bypassing normal approval workflows
Emphasis on secrecy or confidentiality
Communication outside normal business channels
social_engineering.business_impact: Authority-based BEC attacks average $130,000 per incident; healthcare C-suite impersonation leads to HIPAA violations averaging $1.5M in fines.
defensive_measures: Out-of-band verification policy for executive requests
Executive email impersonation protection
Staff training on authority manipulation tactics
Dual-approval requirements for financial transactions

social_engineering.attack_chain: Attacker creates artificial time pressure around court deadlines, patient emergencies, or closing dates to force hasty decisions that bypass security controls.
indicators: Phrases like 'must be done within the hour'
References to imminent deadlines or penalties
Requests to skip verification steps
Unusual after-hours or weekend timing
social_engineering.business_impact: Urgency-driven fraud succeeds 3x more often than standard phishing; average wire fraud loss in legal sector trust account scams is $287,000.
defensive_measures: Mandatory cooling-off period for financial requests over threshold
Automated hold on wire transfers flagged by urgency keywords
Training scenarios using sector-specific deadline pressure

social_engineering.attack_chain: Attacker threatens regulatory action, lawsuit, patient harm, or data exposure to coerce targets into providing credentials, access, or payments.
indicators: Threats of legal action or regulatory penalties
Claims of compromised patient/client data
Demands for immediate payment to avoid consequences
References to specific regulations like HIPAA or bar complaints
social_engineering.business_impact: Fear-based attacks targeting healthcare staff lead to credential compromise in 23% of cases; average remediation cost is $850,000 per incident.
defensive_measures: Clear escalation procedures for threatening communications
Security team hotline for suspicious contacts
Regular phishing simulations using fear-based pretexts
Policy prohibiting credential sharing under any circumstances
social_engineering.source: SANS Social Engineering Survey 2025, HHS OCR Guidance

social_engineering.attack_chain: Attacker researches target via LinkedIn and firm websites, then impersonates a known colleague, client, or vendor using personal details to build false trust.
indicators: References to specific internal projects or cases
Use of personal details not publicly available
Requests from personal email claiming work account issues
Name-dropping of real colleagues or clients
social_engineering.business_impact: Familiarity-based attacks have a 47% click rate versus 12% for generic phishing; average data breach from trusted impersonation costs $3.2M.
defensive_measures: External email banners with sender verification warnings
Social media hygiene training for staff
Client communication verification protocols
Email authentication preventing domain spoofing

social_engineering.attack_chain: Attacker claims that other partners, departments, or peer organizations have already approved or adopted a fraudulent process to pressure compliance.
indicators: Claims that 'everyone else has already done this'
Fake approval chains or forwarded authorization emails
References to peer organizations adopting new procedures
Manufactured consensus in group communications
social_engineering.business_impact: Social proof attacks targeting professional firms succeed 35% more often than isolated requests; average loss per incident is $95,000.
defensive_measures: Independent verification of claimed approvals
Centralized change management for new procedures
Training on social proof manipulation techniques
social_engineering.source: Cialdini Influence Framework, Proofpoint Human Factor Report 2025

healthcare.attack_chain: Ransomware specifically targets EHR systems and clinical databases, encrypting patient records and demanding payment to restore access to care-critical data.
indicators: Encryption of HL7/FHIR data stores
Database service termination commands
Backup deletion targeting EHR backup paths
Ransom notes referencing patient safety
healthcare.business_impact: EHR downtime costs hospitals $47,000 per hour; average ransomware recovery in healthcare takes 23 days at a total cost of $10.9M.
defensive_measures: Air-gapped EHR backups with 4-hour RPO
Network segmentation isolating clinical systems
EDR deployment on all EHR-connected endpoints
Incident response plan with clinical downtime procedures
healthcare.source: HHS HC3 Threat Brief 2025, CISA Healthcare Advisory

healthcare.attack_chain: Legacy medical devices running outdated OS versions are exploited via known CVEs to gain network foothold or disrupt patient monitoring systems.
indicators: Network scans targeting DICOM/HL7 ports
Exploit attempts against known device CVEs
Unauthorized firmware modifications
Anomalous traffic from infusion pumps or imaging systems
healthcare.business_impact: FDA Class I recalls due to cyber vulnerabilities cost manufacturers $5-15M; compromised devices can directly endanger patient safety.
defensive_measures: IoMT asset inventory with automated discovery
Virtual patching via network-based IPS
Medical device network microsegmentation
Manufacturer vulnerability disclosure tracking
Pre-procurement security assessment requirements
healthcare.source: FDA Cybersecurity Guidance 2025, ICS-CERT Medical Advisories

healthcare.attack_chain: Phishing email compromises staff credentials, granting access to patient records in EHR or email, triggering HIPAA breach notification for all affected individuals.
indicators: Bulk patient record access from compromised account
PHI detected in email attachments to external recipients
Login from unusual location using staff credentials
Mass download of patient demographic reports
healthcare.business_impact: HIPAA breach fines range from $100 to $1.9M per violation category; average healthcare breach costs $10.9M including notification, credit monitoring, and OCR settlement.
defensive_measures: Phishing-resistant MFA for all EHR access
Role-based access controls with minimum necessary enforcement
Automated PHI detection in outbound email
HIPAA-specific phishing simulation training
healthcare.source: HHS OCR Breach Portal 2025, HIPAA Journal Analysis

healthcare.attack_chain: Healthcare worker abuses legitimate access to view records of celebrities, coworkers, family members, or acquaintances without treatment relationship or authorization.
indicators: Access to records outside assigned patient population
Repeated views of high-profile patient records
Record access without corresponding appointments or orders
After-hours access to non-emergency patient data
healthcare.business_impact: Insider snooping HIPAA fines average $250,000 per incident; UCLA Health paid $7.5M for repeated celebrity record access violations.
defensive_measures: Proactive audit logging with behavioral analytics
Break-the-glass access controls with mandatory justification
Random audit reviews of patient record access
Clear sanctions policy with termination for violations
healthcare.source: HHS OCR Enforcement Actions, AHLA Compliance Guide

healthcare.attack_chain: Third-party vendor with BAA-protected access to PHI is compromised, exposing patient data from multiple covered entities simultaneously.
indicators: Vendor reports security incident affecting shared systems
Unusual data access patterns from vendor service accounts
PHI appearing on dark web linked to vendor systems
Vendor authentication anomalies
healthcare.business_impact: Business associate breaches affect 3x more records on average than direct breaches; Change Healthcare breach in 2024 affected 100M+ records costing $2.4B+.
defensive_measures: Annual vendor security risk assessments
BAA with specific breach notification SLAs under 24 hours
Vendor access limited to minimum necessary with MFA
Continuous monitoring of vendor connection activity
healthcare.source: HHS OCR Guidance on Business Associates, CHIME Survey 2025

healthcare.attack_chain: Attacker exploits vulnerabilities in telehealth platforms to intercept patient consultations, steal credentials, or inject malicious content into virtual care sessions.
indicators: Unauthorized participants in telehealth sessions
Telehealth platform API abuse
Session hijacking via stolen meeting links
Credential stuffing against patient portal logins
healthcare.business_impact: Telehealth breaches expose both clinical and payment data; average incident costs $3.7M including regulatory fines and patient notification.
defensive_measures: End-to-end encrypted telehealth platforms only
Unique per-session meeting links with waiting rooms
Patient identity verification before clinical discussion
Regular penetration testing of telehealth infrastructure
healthcare.source: HHS Telehealth Privacy Guidance 2025, NIST Telehealth Security Framework

legal.attack_chain: Attacker compromises attorney email, monitors real estate or M&A transactions, then sends fraudulent wire instructions to redirect trust account disbursements.
indicators: Inbox rules hiding client replies
Wire instruction changes near closing dates
Login anomalies on attorney email accounts
Subtle domain spoofing in reply chains
legal.business_impact: Average trust account BEC loss is $287,000; firms face malpractice liability and bar disciplinary action in addition to direct financial loss.
defensive_measures: Mandatory verbal callback for all wire instructions
Trust account reconciliation within 24 hours
Email compromise detection with impossible travel alerts
Client communication policy prohibiting wire changes via email
legal.source: ABA Cybersecurity Report 2025, FBI IC3 Legal Sector Brief

legal.attack_chain: Attacker impersonates senior partner via spoofed email or deepfake voice to instruct associates or staff to transfer funds, share privileged documents, or modify filings.
indicators: Requests from unfamiliar email addresses claiming to be partners
Unusual requests bypassing normal approval chains
Voice calls with slight audio artifacts
Requests for privileged documents outside normal matter workflow
legal.business_impact: Partner impersonation attacks average $175,000 in direct losses; reputational damage and client trust erosion can cost 10x the direct financial loss.
defensive_measures: Out-of-band verification for any partner financial requests
Email authentication preventing display-name spoofing
Deepfake awareness training for administrative staff
Dual-authorization for disbursements above threshold
legal.source: ILTA Technology Survey 2025, ABA Formal Opinion 483

legal.attack_chain: Departing attorney or compromised account is used to exfiltrate privileged client files, merger documents, or litigation strategy to competitors or adverse parties.
indicators: Bulk document downloads from DMS
Large email attachments to personal accounts
USB device usage on restricted workstations
Cloud sync to unauthorized storage services
legal.business_impact: Client data theft exposes firms to malpractice claims averaging $2.1M; breach of attorney-client privilege can result in case dismissal and bar sanctions.
defensive_measures: DLP policies on document management systems
Automated alerts for bulk file access or download
USB port restrictions on all endpoints
Immediate access revocation upon departure notice
Ethical wall enforcement for conflicted matters
legal.source: ABA Model Rules 1.6, ILTA Security Survey 2025

legal.attack_chain: Attacker targets e-discovery platforms or review databases containing concentrated volumes of privileged and sensitive documents from multiple matters.
indicators: Unauthorized access to e-discovery review platforms
Bulk export of document collections
Anomalous API calls to e-discovery tools
Privilege log access by unauthorized users
legal.business_impact: E-discovery breaches expose concentrated privileged data; spoliation sanctions and malpractice exposure can exceed $5M per matter affected.
defensive_measures: Role-based access controls on review platforms
Audit logging for all e-discovery data access
Encryption at rest and in transit for review databases
Multi-factor authentication for e-discovery platform access
legal.source: Sedona Conference Commentary on E-Discovery Security, EDRM Framework

legal.attack_chain: Attacker gains access to the firm's DMS containing all client matters, enabling mass exfiltration of privileged communications, contracts, and litigation files.
indicators: Unusual DMS search queries across multiple matters
Service account abuse accessing document repositories
Large-scale document exports or print jobs
DMS access from unmanaged devices
legal.business_impact: DMS compromise exposes the firm's entire client portfolio; average remediation cost is $8.5M including forensics, notification, and client relationship management.
defensive_measures: Zero-trust access model for DMS with device compliance
Behavioral analytics on DMS access patterns
Ethical walls enforced at the DMS level
Regular access reviews and permission audits
Encrypted document storage with key management
legal.source: ILTA Technology Survey 2025, NetDocuments Security Whitepaper

legal.attack_chain: Attacker impersonates opposing counsel or court personnel to trick firm staff into sharing case strategy, settlement positions, or privileged work product.
indicators: Emails from domains mimicking court or opposing firm
Requests for documents outside normal discovery channels
Phone calls seeking case details without proper identification
Fake court orders demanding immediate document production
legal.business_impact: Disclosure of litigation strategy can result in case losses valued at $1-50M depending on matter; ethical violations may lead to bar discipline and malpractice claims.
defensive_measures: Verification protocols for all external document requests
Staff training on legal-specific social engineering
Centralized intake for court communications
Email banner warnings for external senders mimicking legal domains
legal.source: ABA Cybersecurity Report 2025, State Bar Ethics Opinions
